criteria.profile: Post-apocalyptic, psychological horror, claustrophobic tension, survival
criteria.curation: Deep cuts only -- no mainstream horror

t1_direct_matches.label: Tier 1 -- Direct Matches
t1_direct_matches.description: Closest in tone, setting, and psychological intensity to The Divide

t2_claustrophobic.label: Tier 2 -- Claustrophobic Single-Location
t2_claustrophobic.description: Confined-space psychological tension with group dynamics

t3_survival.label: Tier 3 -- Survival Pressure Cookers
t3_survival.description: High-concept survival scenarios with moral dilemmas

t4_adjacent.label: Tier 4 -- Adjacent Deep Cuts
t4_adjacent.description: Shares DNA with The Divide but diverges in subgenre or setting

movies.title: Right at Your Door
movies.why_similar: Dirty bomb hits LA; husband seals himself inside the house while wife is outside. Same claustrophobic domestic apocalypse tension as The Divide. Paranoia escalates in a sealed space with dwindling trust.

movies.title: Hidden
movies.why_similar: Family hiding in a bunker after an outbreak. Pre-Stranger Things Duffer Brothers. Claustrophobic survival with a twist ending. Same shelter-in-place dread.

movies.title: Carriers
movies.why_similar: Four survivors navigate a pandemic wasteland with strict rules to avoid infection. Moral decay under survival pressure mirrors The Divide's group disintegration. Stars Chris Pine pre-Star Trek fame.

movies.title: It Comes at Night
movies.why_similar: Family boarded up in a house during an unspecified plague. Another family arrives. Trust erodes into paranoia and violence. The quintessential 'who can you trust in the apocalypse' film.

movies.title: Await Further Instructions
movies.why_similar: Family trapped in their house on Christmas Day by a mysterious barricade. TV gives increasingly sinister instructions. Sealed-in paranoia with family tensions boiling over -- very Divide-adjacent.

movies.title: Coherence
movies.why_similar: Dinner party goes wrong when a comet passes overhead. Reality fractures. Shot in a single house with improvised dialogue. The paranoia and group dynamics in a confined space are pure Divide energy.

movies.title: Pontypool
movies.why_similar: Radio DJ trapped in his station as a virus spreads through the English language. Almost entirely one location. The horror is in what you hear, not what you see. Cerebral claustrophobia.

movies.title: The Platform
movies.why_similar: Vertical prison where food descends level by level. Residents on upper floors feast; lower floors starve. Same resource scarcity and moral collapse as The Divide but as brutal social allegory.

movies.title: Exam
movies.why_similar: Eight candidates in a single room for a final job interview. One question, 80 minutes, strict rules. Group dynamics fracture under pressure. Confined-space tension distilled to its essence.

movies.title: The Bar
movies.why_similar: Patrons trapped in a Madrid bar after a sniper kills anyone who exits. Paranoia mounts as they turn on each other. Alex de la Iglesia's darkly comic take on the locked-room survival scenario.

movies.title: Circle
movies.why_similar: 50 strangers in a circle. Every 2 minutes, one dies by group vote. Pure survival psychology with no escape. Strips The Divide's group dynamics to the absolute minimum -- just people and decisions.

movies.title: Would You Rather
movies.why_similar: Desperate guests at a rich man's dinner party forced into increasingly brutal 'would you rather' choices. Confined space, escalating violence, moral compromise under duress.

movies.title: Fermat's Room
movies.why_similar: Four mathematicians locked in a room that shrinks unless they solve puzzles. Literal claustrophobia as survival mechanism. Spanish-language gem with English-friendly accessibility.

movies.title: Level 16
movies.why_similar: Girls in a mysterious boarding school discover the horrifying purpose of their captivity. Institutional claustrophobia with a body-horror twist. Clean, clinical dread.

movies.title: The Incident
movies.why_similar: Two groups trapped in infinite loops -- one in a stairwell, one on a highway. Time passes but space repeats. Existential claustrophobia that gets under your skin. Mexican indie gem.

movies.title: Pandorum
movies.why_similar: Crew wakes on a spaceship with no memory. Something is hunting them in the dark. Claustrophobic corridors, resource scarcity, and psychological breakdown in a sealed environment -- The Divide in space.

movies.title: The Void
movies.why_similar: People trapped in a hospital surrounded by cultists. Cosmic horror seeps in. Practical effects, siege dynamics, and escalating dread in a sealed building. Carpenter-esque.

movies.title: Breathing Room
movies.why_similar: Strangers wake in a locked white room wearing explosive collars. Must figure out the rules or die. Ultra-low-budget but effective confined-space tension. Deep cut that predates the wave.

movies.title: The Hole
movies.why_similar: Four teens voluntarily lock themselves in a WWII bunker for a party weekend. Things go wrong. Early Keira Knightley. The voluntary confinement turning deadly mirrors The Divide's shelter-gone-wrong.

movies.title: The Bunker
movies.why_similar: Survivors in a bunker after nuclear event. Resources dwindle, tensions rise, paranoia sets in. The most direct spiritual successor to The Divide in recent years. Ultra-indie but committed.
